component: repo validation command wrappers and Node warning filtering
impact: The normal validation sequence passed with repeated third-party Node ExperimentalWarning output, making local and CI logs look noisy even when there were no actionable repo failures.
rootCause: Newer Node releases surface ExperimentalWarning messages when CommonJS tooling requires ES modules. The recurring warnings came from npm's debug dependency requiring supports-color and from ESLint's legacy eslintrc loader requiring svelte-eslint-parser; grouped Playwright runs repeated the npm/debug warning for each managed web server startup.
resolution: Added a repo warning preload that runs under --no-warnings and re-emits every warning except the two known third-party CommonJS-to-ESM ExperimentalWarning patterns. Root and frontend npm scripts now receive that preload through .npmrc node-options, while the existing ESLintRCWarning suppression is preserved for explicit ESLint invocations.
verification: npm run lint; npm run type-check; npm run build; npm test; node scripts/link-check.mjs
